message:
perf: eliminate redundant deep clones in agent loop and provider streams

Agent loop (agent.rs):
- Wrap last_assistant in Arc<AssistantMessage> instead of cloning the
  full message each iteration. The agent loop previously deep-cloned the
  entire assistant message (which includes all content blocks, tool calls,
  and thinking content) on every turn just to keep a reference for abort
  handling. Now stores Arc and uses Arc::clone (O(1)), with
  Arc::unwrap_or_clone at the single exit point.
- Change build_abort_message to accept Option<&AssistantMessage> instead
  of Option<AssistantMessage>, avoiding an ownership transfer and clone
  at the abort check site.

Config fast path (main.rs):
- Add early-return fast path for `pi config --paths` that skips the
  async runtime and package collection entirely. This subcommand only
  needs filesystem path resolution, not package metadata.
- Guard collect_config_packages behind a need_packages check so the
  --paths flag doesn't pay for async package enumeration it won't use.

Provider tool call ordering (anthropic.rs, cohere.rs, openai_responses.rs):
- Reorder tool call finalization across all three providers to construct
  the ToolCall event BEFORE updating the partial content block. This
  lets the original parsed_args value move into whichever consumer comes
  last, eliminating one redundant clone per tool call in each provider.
  Previously both the event and the partial block received clones; now
  one gets the original via move.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
